title: Rhinoplasty
shortDescription: Rhinoplasty reshapes the nose for aesthetic or functional improvements. South Korean surgeons are globally recognized for achieving natural, harmonious results tailored to diverse facial structures.
description: Rhinoplasty (nose reshaping surgery) is one of South Korea's most sought-after procedures, with Seoul's Gangnam district home to hundreds of board-certified plastic surgeons who specialize in both cosmetic and functional nasal correction. Korean rhinoplasty techniques emphasize natural results that complement facial harmony — a particularly important consideration for international patients seeking subtle refinement. Procedures range from tip refinement and bridge augmentation to deviated septum correction. Most patients stay 7–10 days in Korea for surgery, initial recovery, and a follow-up check before flying home. Consult a qualified surgeon to determine which technique is appropriate for your anatomy and goals.
priceNote: Estimated cost including basic procedure fees.

title: Double Eyelid Surgery
shortDescription: Double eyelid surgery creates a defined upper eyelid crease, making eyes appear larger and more defined. South Korea performs more of these procedures than any other country, with techniques refined over decades of practice.
description: Double eyelid surgery (blepharoplasty) is South Korea's single most performed cosmetic procedure. Korean surgeons have developed specialized techniques — including the popular non-incision suture method — that produce natural-looking results with minimal downtime. The surgery creates or deepens the supratarsal fold of the upper eyelid. Korean clinics offer both incisional (permanent, suitable for heavier or thicker lids) and non-incisional methods (reversible, faster recovery, suitable for thinner lids). Most patients are discharged the same day, with swelling resolving over 1–2 weeks. A typical stay of 5–7 days allows for surgery and an initial healing review before departure.
priceNote: Estimated cost including basic procedure fees.

title: Facelift
shortDescription: A facelift (rhytidectomy) repositions facial tissues and removes excess skin to reduce visible signs of aging. South Korean plastic surgeons use advanced SMAS-layer techniques that produce natural, long-lasting results without an overly tightened look.
description: Facelift surgery in South Korea addresses sagging facial tissues, deep nasolabial folds, jowling, and loose neck skin through surgical repositioning of the underlying SMAS (Superficial Musculoaponeurotic System) layer. Korean plastic surgeons are known for SMAS-based approaches that avoid the pulled appearance associated with older skin-only techniques. Procedures range from a mini-lift targeting early laxity under local anesthesia to a comprehensive full facelift with neck lift under general anesthesia. Recovery involves swelling and bruising for 2–3 weeks, with most patients presentable at 3–4 weeks. International patients typically stay 10–14 days in Korea to cover surgery and the critical early recovery phase. Consult a board-certified surgeon to determine the appropriate procedure for your degree of skin laxity.
priceNote: Estimated cost including basic procedure fees.

title: Breast Augmentation
shortDescription: Breast augmentation uses implants or fat transfer to increase breast size and improve shape. South Korean plastic surgeons are experienced with a wide range of implant options and emphasize natural, proportional results.
description: Breast augmentation in South Korea is performed using Korean FDA-approved silicone gel implants (round or anatomical) or saline implants, placed either under or over the chest muscle through discreet incisions — typically inframammary, periareolar, or transaxillary. Korean surgeons emphasize proportional results relative to the patient's body frame rather than maximum size. Many clinics also offer fat transfer breast augmentation as an alternative for patients seeking modest, natural enhancement without implants. The procedure is performed under general anesthesia and typically takes 1–2 hours. Most patients resume light activities within 1–2 weeks. International patients should plan 7–10 days in Korea for surgery, initial recovery, and a follow-up assessment.
priceNote: Estimated cost including basic procedure fees.

title: Liposuction
shortDescription: Liposuction removes stubborn fat deposits from targeted body areas using suction techniques. South Korean clinics are experienced in body contouring and frequently combine liposuction with complementary sculpting procedures.
description: Liposuction in South Korea removes diet- and exercise-resistant fat deposits from areas including the abdomen, flanks, thighs, arms, and under the chin using cannula-based suction techniques. Korean clinics commonly use tumescent liposuction, VASER ultrasound-assisted liposuction, or power-assisted liposuction depending on the treatment area and desired outcome. Results are most visible after 3–6 months once post-operative swelling has resolved. The procedure requires wearing compression garments for 4–6 weeks post-operatively. Most patients return to desk work within 1 week. International patients should plan 5–7 days in Korea for surgery and initial recovery monitoring. Liposuction is a body-contouring procedure and is not a substitute for weight management.
priceNote: Estimated cost including basic procedure fees.

title: V-Line Surgery
shortDescription: V-line surgery reshapes the lower face to create an oval or V-shaped jawline. South Korea pioneered this procedure and remains the global center of expertise for facial bone contouring.
description: V-line surgery is a category of facial bone contouring procedures originating in South Korea, designed to reduce jaw width and refine the chin to create a slimmer, more tapered lower face. The most common components are mandible angle reduction (removing the prominent bony angles of the jaw) and genioplasty (chin reshaping). Some patients also combine this with cheekbone reduction for a comprehensive mid and lower face transformation. These are surgical procedures performed under general anesthesia, with a recovery period of 4–6 weeks for major swelling to fully subside — though most patients are presentable within 2 weeks. Korean maxillofacial and plastic surgeons have performed more facial contouring cases than anywhere else in the world. Consult a specialist to confirm candidacy and realistic expectations.
priceNote: Estimated cost including basic procedure fees.

title: Dental Implants
shortDescription: Dental implants are titanium posts surgically placed into the jawbone to support permanent replacement teeth. South Korea offers implants from globally trusted Korean brands (Osstem, Dentium) at significantly lower cost than Western countries.
description: Dental implants in South Korea deliver outstanding value — Korean brands Osstem and Dentium are among the top three implant manufacturers globally, used in clinics worldwide, yet treatments in Korea typically cost 40–60% less than equivalent procedures in the US or UK. A standard implant process involves three stages: implant placement, osseointegration (3–6 months of healing in the jawbone), and crown fitting. For international patients, many Seoul dental clinics offer accelerated immediate-loading protocols where patients receive a temporary crown on the same day as implant placement. Most multi-implant cases can be structured across two visits to Korea, each 5–7 days. Implants are not suitable for patients with uncontrolled diabetes, active gum disease, or insufficient bone density without prior grafting — a comprehensive consultation will determine your candidacy.
priceNote: Estimated cost including basic procedure fees.

title: LASIK Eye Surgery
shortDescription: LASIK uses a laser to reshape the cornea and correct nearsightedness, farsightedness, and astigmatism. South Korea's dedicated refractive eye surgery centers serve large numbers of international patients using the latest femtosecond laser technology.
description: LASIK eye surgery in South Korea is performed at specialized eye surgery hospitals that focus exclusively on refractive procedures, unlike general hospitals that offer it as a secondary service. Korean centers — particularly in Seoul's Gangnam and Yeouido districts — use current-generation femtosecond lasers and wavefront-guided technology for personalized corneal reshaping. A complete LASIK experience in Korea typically involves pre-operative screening (3–4 hours), surgery (approximately 15 minutes per eye), and a follow-up examination the next day. Most patients notice significantly improved vision within 24 hours. International patients generally stay 3–5 days. Suitability for LASIK depends on corneal thickness, prescription range, and overall ocular health — not all patients are candidates. Alternative procedures (LASEK, SMILE) may be recommended based on your individual assessment.
priceNote: Estimated cost including basic procedure fees.

title: Knee Replacement
shortDescription: Knee replacement resurfaces damaged knee joint surfaces with metal and plastic components to relieve chronic pain and restore mobility. South Korean orthopedic hospitals offer robotic-assisted procedures with short hospital stays and structured rehabilitation.
description: Total knee replacement (arthroplasty) in South Korea is performed at orthopedic specialty hospitals that have invested in robotic surgical systems and rapid recovery protocols. The surgery replaces worn cartilage and bone surfaces of the knee joint with fitted prosthetic components, typically providing pain relief and improved function for 15–20 years. Korean hospitals routinely achieve hospital stays of 3–5 days for uncomplicated cases, with physiotherapy beginning the day after surgery. International patients planning knee replacement in Korea should budget 10–14 days in-country to allow for surgery, initial rehabilitation, and a discharge assessment. A structured home physiotherapy program is essential upon return and directly affects long-term outcomes. Consult an orthopedic surgeon to confirm candidacy based on X-rays and functional assessment.
priceNote: Estimated cost including basic procedure fees.

title: IVF Treatment
shortDescription: IVF involves stimulating egg production, retrieving eggs, fertilizing them in a laboratory, and transferring embryos to the uterus. South Korean fertility clinics combine advanced laboratory technology with personalized protocols at costs significantly lower than Western countries.
description: IVF treatment in South Korea is performed at specialized fertility centers whose laboratory environments and embryology expertise meet international standards. A typical IVF cycle takes 3–5 weeks from start to embryo transfer. Many international patients combine an initial consultation and stimulation start on a first visit, then return for egg retrieval and fresh transfer — or opt for a frozen embryo transfer on a subsequent trip. Korean clinics routinely offer add-on technologies including time-lapse embryo monitoring, preimplantation genetic testing, and endometrial receptivity analysis. Costs are typically 50–70% lower than equivalent protocols in Western Europe or North America. IVF outcomes depend on many individual factors including age and ovarian reserve — please consult a qualified fertility specialist to understand realistic expectations for your situation.
priceNote: Estimated cost including basic procedure fees.

title: Egg Freezing
shortDescription: Egg freezing (oocyte cryopreservation) preserves unfertilized eggs for future use. South Korean fertility clinics use vitrification — the most advanced freezing technique — with high post-thaw egg survival rates.
description: Egg freezing using vitrification is widely available at South Korean fertility clinics, offered both as a fertility preservation measure for women facing medical treatment (such as chemotherapy) and as an elective option. The process involves hormone stimulation over approximately 10–14 days, followed by an egg retrieval procedure under mild sedation. Frozen eggs are stored in liquid nitrogen and can be thawed and fertilized when needed. International patients typically plan a 14–18 day stay in Korea to complete the stimulation and retrieval cycle. Outcomes vary by age and individual ovarian reserve — an assessment of your AMH level and antral follicle count before travelling will help set realistic expectations. This information is general in nature; please consult a fertility specialist for guidance specific to your health profile.
priceNote: Estimated cost including basic procedure fees.

title: Comprehensive Health Screening
shortDescription: Comprehensive health screening provides a full medical assessment including blood work, imaging, and specialist evaluations to detect health conditions early. South Korean hospitals offer internationally recognized health check packages covering 80–100+ tests, typically completed in 1–2 days.
description: Comprehensive health screening at South Korean hospitals represents some of the most thorough preventive health assessments available globally. Premium packages include blood and urine analysis, cardiovascular assessments (EKG, echocardiogram, coronary CT), cancer screenings (endoscopy, colonoscopy, low-dose lung CT, tumor markers), body composition analysis, eye and hearing tests, and specialist consultations — all completed within 1–2 days at a dedicated check-up center. Korea's health screening industry is highly systematized, with dedicated facilities using the latest imaging equipment and streamlined workflows. For international patients, particularly those from regions where access to comprehensive diagnostics is limited, these packages offer a valuable opportunity to obtain a thorough health baseline. Packages are customizable based on age, gender, and individual risk factors.
priceNote: Estimated cost including basic procedure fees.

title: Hair Transplant
shortDescription: Hair transplant surgery restores natural hair growth in areas affected by hair loss or thinning. South Korean clinics use advanced FUE and DHI techniques with high graft survival rates and minimal visible scarring.
description: Hair transplant surgery in South Korea attracts patients from Africa, the Middle East, and beyond seeking experienced surgeons at competitive prices. Korean clinics offer Follicular Unit Extraction (FUE) — the gold standard for minimal scarring — as well as DHI (Direct Hair Implantation) for precision graft placement without prior channel creation. Most procedures involve harvesting 1,500–4,000 grafts from the donor area at the back of the scalp and placing them in thinning or bald zones. Korean surgeons pay close attention to natural hairline design suited to each patient's facial proportions. Patients typically remain in Korea for 5–7 days for the procedure and a first post-operative check. Full results are visible at 12–18 months once transplanted follicles complete their growth cycle.
priceNote: Estimated cost including basic procedure fees.

title: Tummy Tuck
shortDescription: A tummy tuck (abdominoplasty) removes excess abdominal skin and tightens stretched muscles to create a flatter midsection. South Korean surgeons routinely combine this procedure with liposuction for comprehensive body contouring results.
description: Abdominoplasty (tummy tuck) in South Korea addresses loose abdominal skin, stretched muscles (diastasis recti), and stubborn lower belly fat that cannot be resolved through diet and exercise. It is particularly sought after following significant weight loss or pregnancy. Korean plastic surgeons typically combine abdominoplasty with liposuction of the flanks and waist for a comprehensive body contouring result. The procedure is performed under general anesthesia; a full tummy tuck involves a hip-to-hip incision placed low enough to be hidden by underwear or swimwear. Most patients experience 2–3 weeks of significant activity limitation. International patients should plan 10–14 days in Korea. Final results, including scar fading, are typically evident at 3–6 months. Consult a plastic surgeon to confirm that abdominoplasty is appropriate for your anatomy and health status.
priceNote: Estimated cost including basic procedure fees.

title: Dental Implant Surgery
shortDescription: Dental implant surgery places titanium fixtures into the jawbone using precision digital planning. Korean dental clinics use locally manufactured world-class implant brands and computer-guided surgical techniques for accurate placement.
description: Dental implant surgery in Korea uses advanced digital planning tools — including CBCT 3D scans and CAD/CAM-guided surgical templates — to place implants with high accuracy. Leading Korean brands Osstem, Dentium, Neobiotech, Megagen, and Genoss are manufactured to ISO standards and carry international CE certification, making them trusted choices in clinics worldwide. The surgical procedure is typically performed under local anesthesia and takes 30–90 minutes per implant. Korean dental clinics routinely handle complex cases including immediate implants in fresh extraction sites and bone grafting for patients with insufficient bone density. Most single-stage cases allow international patients to complete initial treatment in one 7–10 day visit, with crown fitting on a follow-up trip or via a trusted local dentist using Korean-made components.
priceNote: Estimated cost including basic procedure fees.
